assertion_results.text: An error response JSON schema is defined with required fields: error.code, error.message, and requestId
assertion_results.evidence: No formal JSON schema defined. Error format described in prose as '{ error: string, status: number }' with no requestId field and no code field separate from status.

assertion_results.text: The schema supports optional fields for: error.details (structured), error.target (field name), and error.docUrl (documentation link)
assertion_results.evidence: No optional fields defined. Error format is a flat object with only 'error' (string) and 'status' (number). No details, target, or docUrl support.

assertion_results.text: An error code registry lists at least 15 distinct error codes organized by category (validation, auth, not-found, conflict, rate-limit, internal)
assertion_results.evidence: No error code registry. Document lists 6 HTTP status codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 500) with descriptions but no stable string error codes.

assertion_results.text: Example responses are provided for at least 5 different error scenarios: validation, authentication, not-found, rate-limit, and internal
assertion_results.evidence: Only 3 example responses provided: validation (400), not-found (404), and internal (500). Missing authentication and rate-limit examples.

assertion_results.text: Validation error examples show field-level detail with the specific field name, constraint violated, and value received
assertion_results.evidence: Validation error example shows { error: 'Validation failed', status: 400 } with no field-level detail, no constraint information, and no received value.

assertion_results.text: Rate limit error response includes Retry-After header guidance or a retryAfter field in the response body
assertion_results.evidence: No rate limit error response provided. Rate limiting not mentioned in the document.

assertion_results.text: Internal server errors use a generic safe message and never expose implementation details in the example
assertion_results.evidence: Internal error example uses { error: 'Internal server error', status: 500 } which is generic and safe.

assertion_results.text: A developer-facing error reference document explains how clients should programmatically handle each error category
assertion_results.evidence: No developer-facing error reference document. Only a brief section titled 'Error Handling' with a table of status codes and one-line descriptions.
